content.claim: Cross-nationally, early between-school tracking at age 10 (as in Hungary, Austria, Germany) is associated with higher educational inequality and greater impact of social background on achievement, compared to comprehensive systems tracking at 15–16 (Finland, Australia, Poland), with no clear equity-efficiency trade-off.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Meta-analytic literature flagged as 'inconclusive' in 2021 Springer review due to variation in data, domains, and inequality measures
Causal identification relies on quasi-experimental designs sensitive to parallel-trends assumptions
Recent meta-analysis shows strong effects on achievement gaps but smaller effects on other outcomes, suggesting heterogeneous mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
